England.status: Top of group on GD, effectively safe

Panama.status: ELIMINATED — no realistic path to advancement

matches._note: Panama's foul count nearly doubled (11→19) as their possession fell (62%→42%) — clean defending-team-fouls-more signal, and they'll have far less of the ball against England than against either prior opponent.

context_flags: EXTREME_MISMATCH: England 85.11% favorite — comparable in scale to NED-TUN/POR-UZB blowout regime. Panama is mathematically eliminated.

PRIOR_LESSON_RULE15_FAJARDO: We have DIRECT history on this exact player — GHA-PAN Q10, we submitted 0.57 on Fajardo SOT (vs an extreme-underdog discount that should have given 0.25-0.30), he had 0 SOT, cost us -13.8 RBP. Today Panama is an even bigger underdog (4.3% vs ~13% then) — apply RULE15 fully this time, do not anchor on the market-adjusted illiquid price alone.

PRIOR_LESSON_FOULS_PATTERN: ENG-CRO confirmed 'low-foul dominant team' (England fouled less while dominant, +18.82 win). Panama's own foul count already nearly doubled from their highest-possession match (62%, 11 fouls) to their lowest (42%, 19 fouls) — today they'll have less possession than either, reinforcing a high foul count.

SUBMISSION_DISCIPLINE_FLAG: ENG-CRO's single worst process error was a Kane-related submission (typo'd 0.20 instead of 0.43, cost -44.54). Kane appears again today — double check the submitted value against this file before confirming.

questions.text: Will Panama commit more fouls than England?
questions.reasoning: Raw normal-approx off ESPN averages (PAN 15.0 vs ENG 12.0) gives 0.76. Context reinforces rather than contradicts: Panama's foul rate already scales inversely with their possession share (62%→11 fouls, 42%→19 fouls), and they'll see less of the ball against England than against either prior opponent. Direct lesson from ENG-CRO (dominant teams foul less) and CRO-PAN (defending underdogs should be priced higher, not coin-flip) both point the same way.

questions.text: Will there be 4 or more total shots on target in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Combined full-match SOT average (ENG 7.0 + PAN 2.5 = 9.5), half-split λ≈4.75 → raw Poisson P(≥4)=0.70. Trimmed slightly for the chance England eases off once comfortably ahead (seen in other blowout 2H patterns, e.g. CUR-IVC, ARG-AUT).

questions.text: Will Panama score the first goal of the game and England score in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Compound: P(Panama scores first)=0.1482, P(England scores in 2H)=0.8048. Naive product=0.119. Small positive-correlation bump (if Panama somehow scores first, England's chase intensity in 2H only rises further) per the same adjustment used in USA-PAR and TUR-USA.

questions.text: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown?
questions.reasoning: Direct compound market: Penalty Yes=35.48% (one-sided/illiquid but usable), Any sent off Yes=11.10%. P(OR)=1-(0.6452×0.889)=0.4264. This question family has been a reliable source of edge all tournament.

questions.text: Will Panama score at least 1 goal?
questions.reasoning: Direct market: Panama over 0.5 goals = 42.79%. Trust it — Panama has scored 0 goals across 2 WC2026 matches, but the market already reflects some chance of a consolation goal against a rotated/comfortable England backline.

questions.text: At halftime, will the match be tied?
questions.reasoning: Direct HT market: Draw = 25.82%. Trust it.

questions.text: Will the match have 3 or more total goals?
questions.reasoning: Direct market: Over2.5 = 69.23%. England has scored 4+ goals already this tournament; consistent with a comfortable, goal-heavy expected result.

questions.text: Will there be 5 or more total corner kicks in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Combined full-match corner average (ENG 8.5 + PAN 4.5 = 13.0), half-split λ≈6.5 → raw Poisson P(≥5)=0.78. Trimmed down for uncertainty in the even-split assumption — England may front-load corners in 1H if they race to an early lead.

questions.text: Will José Fajardo have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.reasoning: Illiquid offer-adjusted market gives 0.422, but we have a direct, painful prior lesson on this EXACT player: GHA-PAN Q10, we trusted a similar market-adjusted number (0.57) over RULE15, Fajardo had 0 SOT, cost -13.8 RBP, and the postmortem explicitly said 'should have been ~0.25-0.30.' Panama is an even bigger underdog today (4.3% win prob) than in that match. Apply the lesson fully this time.

questions.text: Will Harry Kane have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.reasoning: Liquid-side bid of 0.8065 (no offer side, used as-is). Kane is England's central striker in an 85% favorite blowout against the weakest defense in the group — consistent with a high SOT probability. No RULE15 concern (England is the dominant side). NOTE: double-check this value on submission — Kane was the subject of our single worst-ever submission error (ENG-CRO, -44.54 from a typo).

strongest_picks: Q9: Fajardo SOT = 0.28 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — direct corrective application of a documented prior mistake on this exact player)
Q1: Panama more fouls = 0.72 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — clean possession-foul relationship, reinforced by ENG-CRO and CRO-PAN history)
Q10: Kane SOT = 0.78 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — liquid market, dominant favorite; flagged for submission-accuracy double-check)
